pattern: Working backwards from the destination reveals the minimum health required at each cell without greedily committing to a path early.
hints: Forward DP is hard because the minimum starting health depends on the whole path, not just the current cell; think about what information you really need at each step.
If you know the minimum health needed to survive from cell (i,j) onward, you can compute the minimum health needed to enter that cell given the room's value.
Fill a DP table from bottom-right to top-left: at each cell take the cheaper of the two exits, subtract the room's effect, and clamp to at least 1.

edge_inputs.description: single cell with negative value forces health > 1
single cell with positive value still requires health >= 1
all positive rooms, answer must still be at least 1
single row path, knight forced right
large positive room early does not compensate for fatal ending
bottom-right cell is very negative, answer driven by last cell

stress.description: random grid up to 20x20 with mixed positive and negative values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, size)
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    grid = [[rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
